query: Build a complete table of every RED CARD shown during the GROUP STAGE of the 2026 FIFA World Cup (the group stage ran 11-27 June 2026). Output a markdown table with EXACTLY these columns: Player, Team, Opponent, Minute. One row per red card.
expected: [{"player": "Sphephelo Sithole", "team": "South Africa", "opponent": "Mexico", "minute": "49"}, {"player": "Themba Zwane", "team": "South Africa", "opponent": "Mexico", "minute": "84"}, {"player": "Cesar Montes", "team": "Mexico", "opponent": "South Africa", "minute": "90+2"}, {"player": "Homam Ahmed", "team": "Qatar", "opponent": "Canada", "minute": "33"}, {"player": "Assim Madibo", "team": "Qatar", "opponent": "Canada", "minute": "53"}, {"player": "Tarik Muharemovic", "team": "Bosnia and Herzegovina", "opponent": "Switzerland", "minute": "80"}, {"player": "Miguel Almiron", "team": "Paraguay", "opponent": "Turkey", "minute": "45+3"}, {"player": "Nathan Ngoy", "team": "Belgium", "opponent": "Iran", "minute": "67"}, {"player": "Rebin Sulaka", "team": "Iraq", "opponent": "Senegal", "minute": "13"}, {"player": "Agustin Canobbio", "team": "Uruguay", "opponent": "Spain", "minute": "90"}]

player.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Allow spelling, diacritic and transliteration variants; an unambiguous surname-only match is acceptable.

team.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same national team.

opponent.criterion:

minute.criterion:

query: For the 2026 French Open (Roland-Garros, 24 May - 7 June 2026), list the winner of each of the five main events. Output a markdown table with EXACTLY these columns: Event, Champion, Runner-up, Score. One row per event: Men's Singles, Women's Singles, Men's Doubles, Women's Doubles, Mixed Doubles.
expected: [{"event": "Men's Singles", "champion": "Alexander Zverev", "runnerup": "Flavio Cobolli", "score": "6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7(5), 6-1"}, {"event": "Women's Singles", "champion": "Mirra Andreeva", "runnerup": "Maja Chwalinska", "score": "6-3, 6-2"}, {"event": "Men's Doubles", "champion": "Marcel Granollers / Horacio Zeballos", "runnerup": "Harri Heliovaara / Henry Patten", "score": "6-4, 6-2"}, {"event": "Women's Doubles", "champion": "Katerina Siniakova / Taylor Townsend", "runnerup": "Anna Danilina / Aleksandra Krunic", "score": "6-2, 7-5"}, {"event": "Mixed Doubles", "champion": "Sara Errani / Andrea Vavassori", "runnerup": "Gabriela Dabrowski / Evan King", "score": "4-6, 6-3, [10-4]"}]

event.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same event (e.g. Men's Singles).

champion.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
For doubles, both players must match; allow name order and diacritic variants.

runnerup.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
For doubles, both players must match; allow name order and diacritic variants.

score.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same final-match score; allow set-separator and tiebreak-notation variants (e.g. 6-7(5) vs 6-7(5-7)).

query: List the MAIN COMPETITION award winners of the 2026 (79th) Cannes Film Festival (May 2026). Output a markdown table with EXACTLY these columns: Award, Film, Recipient. One row per award. For an award shared by two recipients, list both in the same row.
expected: [{"award": "Palme d'Or", "film": "Fjord", "recipient": "Cristian Mungiu"}, {"award": "Grand Prix", "film": "Minotaure", "recipient": "Andrei Zviaguintsev"}, {"award": "Jury Prize", "film": "Das Getraumte Abenteuer", "recipient": "Valeska Grisebach"}, {"award": "Best Director", "film": "La Bola Negra; Fatherland", "recipient": "Javier Calvo & Javier Ambrossi; Pawel Pawlikowski"}, {"award": "Best Actress", "film": "Soudain", "recipient": "Virginie Efira & Tao Okamoto"}, {"award": "Best Actor", "film": "Coward", "recipient": "Emmanuel Macchia & Valentin Campagne"}, {"award": "Best Screenplay", "film": "Notre Salut", "recipient": "Emmanuel Marre"}, {"award": "Camera d'Or", "film": "Ben'Imana", "recipient": "Marie-Clementine Dusabejambo"}, {"award": "Short Film Palme d'Or", "film": "Para Los Contrincantes", "recipient": "Federico Luis"}]

award.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same award category; ignore a '(tied)' suffix.

film.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same film; allow original-language vs English-title variants. If two films share the award, both must be present.

recipient.criterion:
It is sufficient if the semantics are approximately the same as the reference answer or if they point to the same entity. There is no need for a word-for-word correspondence.
Same recipient(s). For shared awards all named recipients must match; allow name order and diacritic variants.
